The PySNMP ProjectzRE-mail: Ilya Etingof <etingof@gmail.com> GitHub: https://github.com/etingof/pysnmpz_This MIB module defines objects specific to User Security Model (USM) implementation at PySNMP.)

notDiscoverabler)
discoverabler#
namedValuesr*
Z	readwriteZ
currentzQWhether SNMP engine would support its discovery by responding to unknown clients.)
doNotDiscoverr)
doDiscoverr#
zdWhether SNMP engine would try to figure out the EngineIDs of its peers by sending discover requests.)
passphraser)
masterr#
Z	localizedr(
not-accessibleaR
When configuring USM user, the value of this enumeration determines how the keys should be treated. The default value "passphrase" means that given keys are plain-text pass-phrases, "master" indicates that the keys are pre-hashed pass-phrases, while "localized" stands for pre-hashed pass-phrases mixed with SNMP Security Engine ID value.)
zgThe table of USM users passphrases configured in the SNMP engine's Local Configuration Datastore (LCD).)
PYSNMP-USM-MIB
pysnmpUsmSecretUserNamez4Information about a particular USM user credentials.)
zMThe username string for which a row in this table represents a configuration.)
zCUser's authentication passphrase used for localized key generation.)
z?User's encryption passphrase used for localized key generation.)
readcreatez
Table statuszjThe table of USM users localized keys configured in the SNMP engine's Local Configuration Datastore (LCD).)
pysnmpUsmKeyEntry)
z-User's localized key used for authentication.)
z)User's localized key used for encryption.)
z1User's non-localized key used for authentication.)
z-User's non-localized key used for encryption.)
